You start with that pre-pre-launch, you move into PLC One, the opportunity, PLC Two, which is the transformation, PLC Three, the ownership experience, and then eventually the sales page, the sales video, the sales letter. ## [06:00 - 09:03]

but the reality is I didn't have it when I started. But you can just by starting to publish and putting out great pre-launch content that will show your positioning. Then number four, you always, this is the thing that a lot of people miss is then no matter what you're selling, there's going to be objections. People are going to have objections to what you're talking about, to objections, to how their life can change, to whether it will work for them, to whether it will work in their situation. And you need to talk about those objections. You can't just put your head in the sand and pretend they don't exist because there's always going to be a number of objections. There's usually going to be two to three really big ones and then a bunch of smaller ones. And you don't need to raise and answer every single one of them, but you definitely need to raise and answer the top one or two. ## [12:04 - 14:18]

advanced enough to know when you can break the rules, then you can mention it. And clearly I've done a lot of launches and I, in my first PLC, I do actually mention the product itself. The bullet point, the second one here is, avoid making your videos too long. There's this old saying, your marketing can never be too long, it can only be too boring. And we all tend to get a little boring when we start talking about ourselves and talking about our products and talking about our business. So you have to be really, really careful here. I think that you should, when in your first launch, probably aim at 12 to 18 minutes. It's not gonna be easy. It's harder to make a really short, tight video. Don't say, Jeff did a 25 minute video, so I'm gonna do a 25 minute video. If I could make that video in 23 minutes, I would. If I could make it in 19 minutes, I would. But to fit everything in there, I had to make it that long. And I do a lot of things to keep it moving quick and moving as quickly as I can and make it as tight as I can. So don't make your videos long just to make them long. Make them as short as you can and still tell the entire story.
